images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of StableVLA’s robustness.(a)Robustness comparison on LIBERO benchmark. Triangle marks and bar charts denote performance on clean data and corrupted data (averaged across severities), respectively. StableVLA achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot robustness.(b)Real-world robot deployment on the Pack Doll task under visual corruptions. StableVLA (0.5B) achieves 50% success rate, outperforming both VLA-Adapter (0.5B, 20%) and OpenPI 0.5 (3B, 40%) despite having fewer parameters.
Figure 2:Catastrophic failure cases under level-5 corruption. StableVLA remains robust while baselines degrade significantly.
Figure 3:Feature consistency across VLA processing stages under visual corruptions.

Figure 4:Architecture of the IB-Adapter and StableVLA.We replace the standard linear bottleneck in StableVLA with Fused IB-Adapter. IB-Adapter processes visual tokens𝐗\mathbf{X}via three parallel pathways to enforce subspace filtering:
(1) Covariance Attention: We utilize raw features as keys (dotted line) to preserve original geometric structure. We compute the Gram Matrix (D×DD\times D) to capture global channel correlations.
(2) Sigmoid Gating: A learnable Sigmoid function generates the Attention Map𝐀\mathbf{A}, acting as an independent gate to suppress noise.
(3) Feature transformation: Features undergo non-linear transformation before reconstruction.
(a)Robustness comparison across corruption types on LIBERO. See AppendixDfor details.
(a)Robustness comparison across corruption types on LIBERO. See AppendixDfor details.
(b)Semantic grouping of Fused IB-Adapter features under speckle noise (severity 3 and 5). StableVLA preserves object-centric clusters while baselines degrade.
Figure 6:Visualization of real-world setup.Top: Physical corruptions (Oil and Shelter). Bottom: Initial states of four evaluation tasks.
Figure 6:Visualization of real-world setup.Top: Physical corruptions (Oil and Shelter). Bottom: Initial states of four evaluation tasks.
